reasoning: The primary driver of Bank Reserve balances moving forward will be the replenishment of the Treasury General Account (TGA). During the debt ceiling impasse, the TGA was drawn down from over $800B to roughly $338B in July 2025. With the debt ceiling reinstated and the OBBBA legislation passed in July, the Treasury is expected to rebuild the TGA back to its stated $850B target by issuing debt. Because the Overnight Reverse Repo (ON RRP) facility is already nearly depleted (at approximately $80B), this $500B+ liquidity drain will directly reduce Bank Reserve balances. Furthermore, the Federal Reserve continues to conduct Quantitative Tightening (QT), albeit at a slower pace ($5B/mo for Treasuries), providing an ongoing headwind to reserve levels. September 15th is also a major corporate tax date, which will significantly spike the TGA and drop reserves by the Sept 16th resolution date. Combined, these forces strongly project lower reserve balances than the August 17th baseline. Per instructions, uncertainty has been scaled up for longer horizons, bringing distant probabilities closer to 0.50.
